message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Rooster and the Pig (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 1,753+ reviews; TripAdvisor 399 reviews; Restaurant Guru aggregate ~4,000 — combined 2000+ across major platforms
- Sentiment: Mostly positive — Yelp/Restaurant Guru aggregate near 4.6, TripAdvisor 4.4. Mild recent mixed signal in May 2026 reviews citing service/order errors at peak times, offset by continued strong food praise.
- Longevity: Opened April 2015; ~11 years operating under continuous ownership of restaurateur Tai Spendley
- Credentials: ["USA Today Restaurant of the Year 2026 (per discovery sources themusehotelpalmsprings.com and visitpalmsprings.com) [UNVERIFIED on USA Today primary source]","TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice award","Vogue feature (April 2017, Coachella dining guide)","Palm Springs Life feature (April 2015 + ongoing best-of inclusions)","Desert Sun feature (June 2015)","CV Independent 'Indy Endorsement' (April 2015)","The Infatuation review"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces consistently for: 'best Vietnamese Palm Springs', 'best restaurants Palm Springs', 'where to dine Palm Springs', and Coachella-week dining roundups. Does NOT reliably surface for: 'date night Palm Springs' (Workshop, Il Corso, Eight4Nine dominate), 'fine dining Palm Springs' (loses to Workshop Kitchen + Bar), or generic 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' (diluted by El Paseo and resort venues). Notably absent from several 2026 'downtown Palm Springs best restaurants' agent-summarized lists despite the USA Today recognition.
- Site structured data: Own website (roosterandthepig.com) is a brochure-style site. No schema.org Restaurant markup detected in extracted content. Hours not posted on landing page. Menu is present but not in machine-readable structured format. Address and phone are present as plain text. Press page is a static list with no article schema. Notable agent-readability gap for a restaurant of this stature.
- Visibility gap score: 7
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Links: [{"title":"Where to Dine in Palm Springs: Best Restaurants 2026","url":"https://www.themusehotelpalmsprings.com/post/where-to-dine-palm-springs"},{"title":"ROOSTER AND THE PIG - Updated May 2026 - 2072 Photos & 1753 Reviews - 356 S Indian Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, California - Vietnamese - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Menu - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/rooster-and-the-pig-palm-springs-7"},{"title":"Rooster and the Pig (2026) – Best of TikTok, Instagram & Reddit Food Guide","url":"https://www.airial.travel/restaurants/united-states/greater-palm-springs/rooster-and-the-pig-Q3CAgSqM"},{"title":"ROOSTER AND THE PIG, Palm Springs - Restaurant Reviews, Photos & Phone Number - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32847-d8316930-Reviews-Rooster_and_the_Pig-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Rooster and the Pig","url":"https://www.roosterandthepig.com/"},{"title":"Rooster and the Pig","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/listing/rooster-and-the-pig/567/"},{"title":"Rooster and the Pig - Vietnamese restaurant · Palm Springs","url":"https://www.postcard.inc/places/rooster-and-the-pig-palm-springs"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about Rooster and the Pig in Palm Springs:

Rooster and the Pig at 356 S Indian Canyon Drive was named USA Today's Restaurant of the Year in 2026, the highest national recognition a Palm Springs restaurant has received in recent memory.

The restaurant serves Vietnamese-Californian cuisine with a tight, rotating menu built around seasonal ingredients. Signature dishes include shrimp and pork spring rolls, spicy chile okra, shaking beef, and caramelized butternut squash with pepitas and pineapple sauce.

A small serving of porridge arrives at every table automatically, which catches first-time visitors off guard in the best way. Reservations are recommended but not technically required.

Links: [{"title":"ROOSTER AND THE PIG - Updated May 2026 - 2072 Photos & 1753 Reviews - 356 S Indian Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, California - Vietnamese - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Menu - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/rooster-and-the-pig-palm-springs-7"},{"title":"press | new-r-and-p - Rooster and the Pig","url":"https://www.roosterandthepig.com/press"},{"title":"The Owner of Rooster and the Pig Shares His Past and Present Story in Food","url":"https://localemagazine.com/the-owner-of-rooster-and-the-pig-shares-his-past-and-present-story-in-food/"},{"title":"Rooster and the Pig Serves Up Vietnamese Food for Greater Palm Springs Area | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/rooster-and-the-pig-serves-up-vietnamese-food-for-greater-palm-springs-area/"},{"title":"ROOSTER & THE PIG Information","url":"https://rocketreach.co/rooster-the-pig-profile_b7e04cc1c068e3c6"},{"title":"about us | new-r-and-p - Rooster and the Pig","url":"https://www.roosterandthepig.com/when-what-where"},{"title":"Rooster and the Pig Restaurant Palm Springs CA Reviews | GAYOT","url":"https://www.gayot.com/restaurants/rooster-pig-palm-springs-ca-92262_35ps160103.html"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Rooster and the Pig in Palm Springs:

Rooster and the Pig was established in 2015. Tai Spendley, making his cooking debut in Palm Springs, crafts a straight-forward, yet progressive menu drawn from his culinary influences: French technique, Asian heritage, and bi-coastal gallivanting.

The restaurant is a 50-seat restaurant tucked into the back corner of a small strip mall on Indian Canyon. Spendley chose the name based on an "Asian Astrology book that claimed that the signs of Rooster and Pig are a force to be reckoned with when combined in work."

After decades working in "front of house" senior level food & beverage roles at trendy hotels and "hot-spot" restaurants in Chicago and Palm Springs, Spendley wanted to return to his roots and opened his first solo concept in the spirit of small, back-alley, authentic restaurants he loves to visit when he returns home to Vietnam.

input.prompt: List all press mentions, awards, and credentials with dates and publications

content.content:
# Press Mentions & Coverage

**Travel and Leisure** – Palm Springs Travel Guide
- Feature on Vietnamese restaurant's fresh ingredients and innovative menu items

**Vogue** – April 2017
- "The Best Places to Eat and Drink in Palm Springs During Coachella"
- Jennifer Conrad highlighted Chef Tai Spendley's modern Southern California approach to Vietnamese cuisine

**Desert Sun** – June 2015
- "The Restaurant Locals Don't Want You to Know About"
- Everett Cook covered the 50-seat establishment's quick rise in local popularity following its April opening

**Palm Springs Life Magazine** – April 2015
- Lori Cohen-Sanford profiled Spendley's return to his culinary roots after decades in hotel and restaurant management

**CV Independent** – April 2015
- Jimmy Boegle's feature "New: Rooster and the Pig"
- Noted the chef's influences as "French technique, Asian heritage and bi-coastal gallivanting"

Web search results for query: ""Rooster and the Pig" "James Beard" OR "Michelin" award nomination"

Don't Miss Pig Island NYC 2024","url":"https://www.nycbbq.com/blog/2024/8/9/best-pork-ever-dont-miss-pig-island-nyc-2024"},{"title":"James Beard and Michelin-Starred Restaurants in Our Featured Destinations | The 100 Collection™","url":"https://theonehundredcollection.com/travel/james-beard-michelin-star-restaurants"},{"title":"Chicago’s James Beard Award-winning restaurants: Where to go and what to expect - Chicago Sun-Times","url":"https://chicago.suntimes.com/food-drink/2025/06/11/chicago-james-beard-award-winning-restaurants"},{"title":"His Restaurant Earned Michelin Recognition — But Survival Came Long Before the Awards","url":"https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/his-restaurant-earned-michelin-recognition-but-survival/id1603232683?i=1000750099674"},{"title":"20+ Michelin Stars, Two James Beard Awards — Then Flies and Bad Fish at Chef Palmer's NYC Rooftop Bar","url":"https://hoodline.com/2026/04/20-michelin-stars-2-james-beard-awards-then-flies-bad-fish-at-chef-palmers-nyc-rooftop-bar/"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about **Rooster & the Till** (not "Rooster and the Pig") in Tampa, Florida:

The restaurant is co-owned by Chef Ferrell Alvarez, who was a 2017 James Beard Foundation semi-finalist in the Best Chef South category. Additionally, Rooster & the Till is a two-time Bib Gourmand recipient (Bib Gourmand is a Michelin recognition for good food at moderate prices).

The search results show that while the restaurant's owner has James Beard recognition, the restaurant itself appears to be primarily recognized through Michelin's Bib Gourmand designation rather than a full Michelin star or additional James Beard awards.

Note: The search didn't find a restaurant specifically called "Rooster and the Pig" — the results show "Rooster & the Till" in Tampa. If you were looking for information about a different restaurant with that exact name, please let me know and I can search again.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Rooster and the Pig in Palm Springs on TripAdvisor:

## Overall Rating and Ranking
Rooster and the Pig is rated 4.4 of 5 on TripAdvisor and ranked #29 of 308 restaurants in Palm Springs, with 397 unbiased reviews.

## Food Quality
Reviewers consistently praise the food. The food was described as amazing, with one reviewer noting it was one of the best meals they had on their 3-week California trip, and everything was unbelievably tasty with substantial portions. One recent reviewer (February 2024) found the food fantastic, particularly praising the chicken dumplings and shaking beef.

## Reservations and Wait Times
An important characteristic of this restaurant is its policy. It's not possible to book, so you need to turn up, give your name to the host and wait to be called, with the waiting area essentially being the car park. As a local Vietnamese place open only 4 days per week with no reservations, diners should expect to wait for a table unless they go very early.

## Service and Atmosphere
One reviewer highlighted that the hospitality was top notch and described as hard to find nowadays. Another noted the food was amazing, service was great, and the atmosphere was lively despite being in a strip mall.

## Mixed Experiences
While most reviews are positive, there are some negative reviews about service and quality changes over time, though these appear to be less common than the glowing recommendations.

Based on the search results, I found information about Rooster and the Pig in Palm Springs:

## Restaurant Overview

Rooster and the Pig was established in 2015. Chef Tai Spendley crafts a straightforward yet progressive menu drawn from his culinary influences: French technique, Asian heritage, and bi-coastal gallivanting.

## Rating

Based on 774 reviews, the restaurant has received a rating of 4.5 stars.

## Cuisine and Concept

It's a stylish, industrial-chic eatery serving ambitious riffs on Vietnamese fare, plus craft cocktails. The restaurant has been recognized by USA Today as one of the Top 47 Restaurants in the U.S. for 2025.

## Dining Experience

Rooster and the Pig doesn't take reservations—but that's part of the charm. The restaurant is located at 356 S Indian Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92262. According to the search results, the restaurant is popular and often has wait times, particularly during peak dining hours.

Based on the search results, I found information about Rooster and the Pig in Palm Springs, California and how TripAdvisor's Travelers' Choice award works.

Tripadvisor gives a Travelers' Choice award to accommodations, attractions and restaurants that consistently earn great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties on Tripadvisor. 

The search results show that Rooster and the Pig is a Vietnamese restaurant located at 356 S Indian Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA. According to the TripAdvisor listing, the restaurant is ranked #24 of 281 Restaurants in Palm Springs and features Asian and Vietnamese cuisine.

The restaurant appears to be well-regarded by travelers, with reviews highlighting the quality of its Vietnamese fusion dishes and generous portions, though it's known for having wait times since it doesn't accept reservations.

content.text: Now I have clarity. The actual recognition is **USA Today's 2025 Restaurants of the Year** (announced ~Feb 2025) — NOT a 2026 award. The claim of "USA Today Restaurant of the Year 2026" is incorrect — it should be on the 2025 list (44 restaurants).

confirms: Opened April 2015 - confirmed via multiple sources (Palm Springs Life April 2015, CV Independent April 2015, Desert Sun June 2015)
Continuous ownership by Tai Spendley - confirmed via Locale Magazine and palmspringslife.com profiles; ~11 years operating
Yelp review volume ~1,753 reviews - confirmed via Yelp page title text
TripAdvisor ~399 reviews and 4.4 rating - confirmed (current count 397, rating 4.4, ranked #29 of 308)
Vogue feature April 2017 (Coachella dining guide) - confirmed on press page
Palm Springs Life feature April 2015 - confirmed on press page
Desert Sun feature June 2015 - confirmed on press page
CV Independent 'Indy Endorsement' April 2015 - confirmed on press page (Jimmy Boegle)
Recognition by USA Today - confirmed but with wrong year/wrong specific award (see contradicts)
contradicts: Claim: 'USA Today Restaurant of the Year 2026' - CONTRADICTED. Independent sources (Facebook post, multiple secondary sources) consistently identify the recognition as USA Today's '2025 Restaurants of the Year' list (44 restaurants, announced Feb 2025), NOT a 2026 award and NOT 'Restaurant of the Year' (singular). One source describes it as 'Top 47 Restaurants in the U.S. for 2025.' The 2026 USA Today Restaurants of the Year list (39 restaurants) does not appear to include Rooster and the Pig based on available coverage. The themusehotelpalmsprings.com and other 2026 dining-guide secondary sources appear to have garbled the year of the original recognition.
flagged_as_unverified: TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice award - NOT verified. No independent source confirms Rooster and the Pig has received this specific award; the restaurant's TripAdvisor listing was not directly accessible to confirm a Travelers' Choice badge.
Restaurant Guru aggregate ~4,000 reviews and ~4.6 rating - not independently verified in this pass.
Yelp aggregate rating near 4.6 - one secondary source cited 4.5 stars (with 774 reviews, which conflicts with the 1,753 figure); direct Yelp access was blocked (HTTP 403). Rating tier unverified.
The Infatuation review - not independently confirmed in this search pass.
Mild recent mixed signal in May 2026 reviews citing service/order errors - not independently verified; TripAdvisor surfaces some mixed reviews historically but no specific May 2026 trend confirmed.
Travel + Leisure feature - listed on press page but no date or specific article confirmed.
input.notes: Core history (April 2015 opening, Tai Spendley continuous ownership, ~11 years) is solidly verified through multiple primary-adjacent sources. Press credentials from 2015-2017 (Palm Springs Life, Desert Sun, CV Independent, Vogue) all confirmed on the restaurant's own press page. No James Beard or Michelin recognition was claimed — and none was found, which is appropriate. The most significant problem: the headline credential 'USA Today Restaurant of the Year 2026' is materially incorrect. The actual recognition is being on USA Today's '2025 Restaurants of the Year' list (a list of ~44 restaurants), announced February 2025. This is a meaningful distinction — it is not 'Restaurant of the Year' (singular national title) and it is not from 2026. The brief should be corrected to: 'USA Today 2025 Restaurants of the Year list inductee.' Also, the TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice claim should be removed or verified — no evidence found for it.
